message:
The other day, my kids were bickering about whose turn it was to choose the movie for family movie night. As I watched them squabble over popcorn and remote control, I couldn’t help but chuckle. Then it hit me—if they can argue about something as trivial as movie choices, how would they handle a real emergency? 

So, I decided to turn our movie night into a little ‘preparedness family meeting’. We talked about what goes into our go-bags, what documents we should have ready, and even made a plan for our family if something were to happen. My kids actually got into it! It felt good to see them shift from playful bickering to teamwork, and I think they even enjoyed being part of the conversation. 

It reminded me that preparedness can be a family affair, and it doesn't have to be all serious—there's room for fun, too! This little refresher helped me feel more at ease. Have you ever turned a mundane moment into a teachable one with your kids?
